title: Rotes Rathaus - Quick Summary
items: **The Rotes Rathaus is red because of its red-brick facade,** not because of communism.
**It was built from 1861 to 1869** by Hermann Friedrich Waesemann, long before East Germany existed.
**The terracotta frieze is a stone history book:** 36 panels tell Berlin history from the 12th century onward.
**The Cold War myth makes sense,** because the building later became East Berlin's city hall in the Soviet sector.
**Since 1991 it has again been Berlin's unified seat of government,** home to the Governing Mayor and Senate Chancellery.
